questions.question: In what year did the U.S. Army establish the Aeronautical Division within the Office of the Chief Signal Officer, marking the military's first formal commitment to aviation?
Who made the first sustained, controlled, heavier-than-air powered flight at Kitty Hawk, North Carolina, on 17 December 1903?
What was the primary role assigned to aircraft during the early years of World War I?
Brigadier General Billy Mitchell is best known for his advocacy of what concept, which he demonstrated by sinking captured warships from the air in 1921?
options: Tactical air support for ground forces
Integrating naval aviation with carrier operations
Independent airpower capable of striking strategic targets

questions.question: What happened to Brigadier General Billy Mitchell as a result of his outspoken criticism of senior military leaders regarding airpower?
options: He was promoted to Major General
He was appointed Secretary of War
He was given command of an independent air corps

questions.question: The Air Corps Act of 1926 changed the name of the Air Service to what?
Which organization, created in June 1941, gave the air arm greater autonomy under the War Department and was commanded by General Henry 'Hap' Arnold?
General Henry 'Hap' Arnold is recognized as holding what distinction in the history of American airpower?
options: First pilot to break the sound barrier
Commanding General of the Army Air Forces during WWII and the only five-star General of the Air Force
First military aviator to fly combat missions in WWI

questions.question: The Tuskegee Airmen were members of what Army Air Forces unit that distinguished itself in combat during World War II?
What was the significance of the Tuskegee Airmen during World War II?
options: They were the first African American military aviators in the U.S. armed forces and compiled an outstanding combat record
They pioneered the development of radar-guided bombing techniques
They were the first military unit to use jet aircraft in combat
They executed the first airborne paratrooper assault in the European Theater

questions.question: In April 1942, Lieutenant Colonel Jimmy Doolittle led a bombing raid on Tokyo using B-25 Mitchell bombers launched from what?
options: A forward airfield on Iwo Jima
A base in the Aleutian Islands

questions.question: What was the primary strategic purpose of the Doolittle Raid on Japan in April 1942?
options: To gain air superiority over the Pacific
To eliminate the Japanese Navy's carrier fleet
To boost American morale and demonstrate that Japan was vulnerable to air attack

questions.question: The Combined Bomber Offensive (CBO) during World War II involved what coordinated strategy?
options: Combined U.S. Navy and Army Air Forces attacks on Japanese island fortifications
Joint Allied bombing of Italian rail networks
U.S. daylight precision bombing and British nighttime area bombing of German targets
American and Soviet coordinated attacks on the Eastern Front

questions.question: Which World War II air campaign targeted German aircraft factories, oil refineries, and transportation networks to cripple the enemy's war-making capability?
General Curtis LeMay directed the strategic bombing campaign against Japan in the Pacific, transitioning from high-altitude daylight bombing to what tactic that proved far more effective?
The atomic bombs dropped on Hiroshima and Nagasaki in August 1945 were delivered by aircraft from which unit?
What legislation established the United States Air Force as an independent service, separate from the Army?
On what date did the United States Air Force officially become an independent branch of the armed forces?
Who served as the first Secretary of the Air Force following the establishment of the USAF as an independent service?
The Berlin Airlift (1948-1949) demonstrated the strategic value of airpower by accomplishing what?
options: Destroying the Soviet blockade through aerial bombardment
Evacuating Allied forces from West Berlin before the blockade
Transporting ground combat troops to reinforce the Berlin garrison
Sustaining West Berlin's population by flying in food, fuel, and supplies over the Soviet blockade

questions.question: What Cold War organization was responsible for maintaining America's nuclear deterrent force and long-range bombing capability?
During the Korean War, 'MiG Alley' referred to the airspace along the Yalu River where American pilots fought against what aircraft?
Which American fighter aircraft achieved a favorable kill ratio against the MiG-15 during the Korean War?
Operation Rolling Thunder (1965-1968) was a sustained bombing campaign during the Vietnam War aimed at what objective?
options: Gaining air superiority over China's southern border
Coercing North Vietnam to stop supporting the insurgency in South Vietnam
Eliminating the Ho Chi Minh Trail through carpet bombing

questions.question: Why was Operation Rolling Thunder considered largely ineffective during the Vietnam War?
options: The Air Force lacked sufficient aircraft to sustain the campaign
North Vietnam had superior air defenses that shot down most U.S. aircraft
The campaign was too short in duration to achieve results
Gradual escalation and political restrictions on targeting limited its strategic impact

questions.question: Operation Linebacker II, the 'Christmas Bombings' of December 1972, helped compel North Vietnam to return to peace negotiations. What type of aircraft played a central role in this operation?
Which general led Strategic Air Command during the early Cold War period and built it into the dominant nuclear deterrent force?
During Operation Desert Storm in 1991, what stealth aircraft struck heavily defended targets in Baghdad on the opening night of the air campaign?
Operation Desert Storm demonstrated the effectiveness of precision-guided munitions (PGMs). What percentage of munitions used in the air campaign were PGMs?
What key lesson did Operation Desert Storm reinforce about the importance of airpower?
options: Achieving air superiority early in a campaign enables dominance across all domains
Airpower could not operate effectively in desert environments
Ground forces alone could have liberated Kuwait without air support
Stealth technology was unreliable in combat conditions

questions.question: Operation Allied Force (1999) over Kosovo was significant because it demonstrated what about airpower?
options: Airpower was ineffective without a ground invasion
An air campaign alone could coerce an adversary to capitulate without a ground offensive
Strategic bombing required nuclear weapons to be effective
Only carrier-based aviation was useful in European conflicts

questions.question: Following the terrorist attacks of September 11, 2001, the Air Force supported Operation Enduring Freedom in which country?
Operation Iraqi Freedom, launched in March 2003, featured what airpower concept that integrated simultaneous air and ground operations from the campaign's outset?
The Global War on Terrorism demonstrated the growing importance of which airpower capability for tracking and engaging insurgent and terrorist targets?
options: Intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR) using remotely piloted aircraft

questions.question: The 2018 National Defense Strategy marked a significant shift in U.S. defense priorities from counterterrorism to what?
options: Great power competition, primarily with China and Russia
Reducing the size of the active duty force

questions.question: The United States Space Force was established as an independent branch of the armed forces in what year?
Under what legislation was the United States Space Force established?
options: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2020

questions.question: Who was the first Chief of Staff of the newly independent United States Air Force in 1947?
What was the Aeronautical Division's first military aircraft, purchased from the Wright Brothers in 1909?
options: Signal Corps Airplane No. 1 (Wright Military Flyer)

questions.question: The Air Service was established as a combatant arm of the U.S. Army in 1918 and saw its first major combat operations during which conflict?
